methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Voyant Photonics is a Series A chip and semiconductor company that has raised $23M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private peers in the same sector and stage, along with 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD). Key private comparables include Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $23M total raised as a proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $46M. Peer-median analysis across the four named private comparables produces a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Voyant's revenue is not on file; while the public semiconductor sector trades at a 25.3x median price-to-sales, private companies rarely disclose revenue.

The triangulated range implied by these two methods spans $39M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $773M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression yields a conservative floor based solely on capital deployed, while peer-median suggests substantially higher value if Voyant's competitive position and market opportunity align with recent frontier-chip funding rounds. The key uncertainty is whether Voyant's technology differentiation and market traction support a valuation toward the peer median or materially below it.
